plain_english: Wins with timing and anticipation from structure
use_when: Use for quarterbacks who consistently throw on time before windows fully open
avoid_when: Do not use for toolsy creators who rely mostly on late reaction playmaking
sample_phrase: Works from the pocket with anticipation and rhythm to beat leverage before the window closes.

plain_english: Can win from rhythm and create outside structure when needed
use_when: Use when the player shows both timing value and controlled off-script playmaking
avoid_when: Do not use when off-script is the only real source of value
sample_phrase: Can stay on schedule from structure and still salvage plays when the pocket moves off the spot.
notes: Good bridge phrase for modern starters

plain_english: Pressure response breaks down under NFL stress
use_when: Use when pressure handling and sack creation remain clear issues
avoid_when: Do not use if pressure response is actually a strength
sample_phrase: Pressure response still runs hot and cold
notes: which can compress the margin for error versus NFL speed.

plain_english: Sees the picture early and gets the ball out on time
use_when: Use when anticipation
avoid_when: coverage recognition
sample_phrase: and timing are real strengths
notes: Do not use when the profile is mostly late-reaction playmaking
column12: Processing speed shows up in how quickly he gets the coverage picture sorted and delivers on time.

plain_english: Operates faster on tape than a stopwatch alone would suggest
use_when: Use when the player processes and reacts fast enough to beat timing windows
avoid_when: Do not use as a synonym for raw athleticism
sample_phrase: Plays faster than he times because he sees it early and reacts on schedule.
notes: Use to separate function from testing

plain_english: Polished profile with limited elite upside
use_when: Use for older or physically capped passers who project as steady pros
avoid_when: Do not use publicly as a lazy downgrade for any non-elite athlete
sample_phrase: The profile is polished and projectable
notes: but the physical upside does not leave much margin for star growth.

plain_english: Creates extra yards through balance and contact power
use_when: Use when missed tackles forced and yards after contact drive the profile
avoid_when: Do not use for pure speed backs without real contact value
sample_phrase: Creates hidden yards through balance

plain_english: Generates chunk plays when he gets daylight
use_when: Use when explosive rate and breakaway profile are strong
avoid_when: Do not use for grinders without home-run juice
sample_phrase: Explosive run ability changes angles once he gets into open grass.

plain_english: Adds value on passing downs as a receiver or space player
use_when: Use when routes
avoid_when: targets per route
sample_phrase: or receptions matter
notes: Do not use for backs with no passing-down role
column12: Passing-down utility gives the role more staying power than a pure early-down grinder.

plain_english: Movement skill shows up in pads and through contact
use_when: Use when burst and flexibility translate better on tape than in pure timed testing
avoid_when: Do not use for track-speed-only backs who do not create in traffic
sample_phrase: Functional athleticism shows up once he has to make bodies miss in tight quarters and stay balanced through contact.

plain_english: Stays on line and keeps momentum through contact
use_when: Use when the back consistently falls forward and finishes runs
avoid_when: Do not use if the runner gets knocked off course by first contact
sample_phrase: Forward body lean keeps the run alive and turns marginal space into efficient carries.

plain_english: Separates with pacing
use_when: leverage manipulation
avoid_when: and break mechanics
sample_phrase: Use when route precision and man/zone efficiency are strengths
notes: Do not use for straight-line field stretchers without route nuance
column12: Route craft shows up in how he attacks leverage
column14: and exits breaks without wasting steps.

plain_english: Pushes coverage vertically and changes spacing structure
use_when: Use when deep role and efficiency are real
avoid_when: Do not use for short-area slot-only profiles
sample_phrase: Vertical stress forces corners to turn and gives the offense more field to work with.

plain_english: Earns throws consistently through separation and trust
use_when: Use when targets per route and YPRR support it
avoid_when: Do not use for manufactured-touch profiles only
sample_phrase: Earns targets because he gets open on time and gives quarterbacks clean windows.

plain_english: Release package versus press is still inconsistent
use_when: Use when press handling remains projection-sensitive
avoid_when: Do not use when release package is already a strength
sample_phrase: Release plan versus press still needs more answers when defenders force timing disruption at the line.

plain_english: Uses the chest and frame instead of consistently plucking the ball
use_when: Use when the receiver repeatedly lets the ball into his body and finish consistency suffers
avoid_when: Do not use if the catch technique is mostly clean and occasional body catches are situational
sample_phrase: Hands finish can get inconsistent because he will let the ball into his frame instead of snatching it cleanly away from his body.
notes: Keep private unless you have strong film support

plain_english: Sudden mover with quick-twitch release and transition skill
use_when: Use when short-area burst and route acceleration are real separators
avoid_when: Do not use for long-striders who win mostly with buildup speed
sample_phrase: Twitch shows up in the release and at the top of routes where he can snap defenders off balance.
notes: Use for separators and dynamic space players

plain_english: Can align attached or detached and still threaten leverage
use_when: Use when the tight end shows real versatility
avoid_when: Do not use for pure in-line blockers or big-slot specialists only
sample_phrase: Alignment versatility lets him stress matchups as both an attach point and a detached target.

plain_english: Needs more consistent lower-half strength to hold the point in-line
use_when: Use when attached blocking remains a projection question
avoid_when: Do not use when play strength is already a positive
sample_phrase: Anchor point in the run game still needs more consistency if he is going to live attached in heavier personnel.

plain_english: Anchor can soften versus NFL power
use_when: Use when bull-rush and strength concerns remain
avoid_when: Do not use if anchor is already a selling point
sample_phrase: Anchor consistency versus NFL power is still the cleanest floor variable in pass protection.

plain_english: Recovers balance and hand position against counters and stunts
use_when: Use when recovery skill stands out on tape
avoid_when: Do not use if the blocker survives mostly by size
sample_phrase: Recovers inside-out well enough to stay connected when rushers change the picture late.

plain_english: Holds ground against power rushes and keeps the pocket firm
use_when: Use when lower-body strength and base control are real strengths
avoid_when: Do not use when the blocker is getting walked back by bull rushers
sample_phrase: Anchor shows up when power rushers try to collapse him and the pocket does not move.
notes: Direct glossary term safe for public

plain_english: Height
use_when: weight
avoid_when: and speed baseline used as a physical threshold check
sample_phrase: Use privately when discussing body-type fit and baseline traits
notes: Do not surface on public cards because it reads too process-heavy
column12: The body-type baseline checks enough boxes to stay in the prototype conversation.

plain_english: Controls contact and wins the first collision
use_when: Use when the profile consistently holds or resets the line of scrimmage
avoid_when: Do not use for purely penetrative players who lose too much contact balance
sample_phrase: Point-of-attack strength gives him a cleaner floor because he can absorb force and still stay in the fit.

plain_english: Creates backfield stress with burst and penetration
use_when: Use when interior pass-rush and penetration are the selling point
avoid_when: Do not use for pure anchor noses
sample_phrase: Works best attacking a gap and stressing protection angles with first-step quickness.

plain_english: Takes on blocks squarely before discarding to finish the rep
use_when: Use when block deconstruction is a clear strength
avoid_when: Do not use for gap-shooters who rarely control blocks cleanly
sample_phrase: Can stack and shed at the point of attack instead of getting stuck on contact.

plain_english: Wins on real pass sets with burst and counters
use_when: Use when true-pass-set win data supports it
avoid_when: Do not use when sack totals are inflated by scheme or manufactured rushes
sample_phrase: The rush profile holds up on true pass sets
notes: which is the cleanest sign of NFL translatable pressure value.

plain_english: Converts wins into real disruption and finish
use_when: Use when pressure converts into sacks
avoid_when: hits
sample_phrase: and clean finish at a healthy rate
notes: Do not use for empty-pressure profiles
column12: Finishes once he clears the edge and closes cleanly on the pocket.

plain_english: Rush wins can flatten if counters do not develop
use_when: Use when pressure exists but counter menu is thin
avoid_when: Do not use when hand usage and counter depth are already strengths
sample_phrase: Rush-plan depth still needs work so NFL tackles cannot sit on the first answer.

plain_english: Can reduce surface and corner without losing speed
use_when: Use when hip/ankle flexibility lets the rusher run the arc cleanly
avoid_when: Do not use for power-only rushers who win through chest-to-chest force
sample_phrase: Bend lets him flatten to the quarterback without giving away speed or balance through the turn.

plain_english: Forces runs back inside and keeps the perimeter clean
use_when: Use when early-down run integrity is a real part of the value
avoid_when: Do not use when the defender gets widened or displaced too often
sample_phrase: Can set the edge and force the ball back into help instead of giving the offense easy width.

plain_english: Controls run fits with key recognition and physicality
use_when: Use for stack linebackers with a strong run-defense profile
avoid_when: Do not use for overhang or coverage-first linebackers
sample_phrase: Run-fit value comes from key discipline
column12: and the ability to meet blocks without getting displaced.

plain_english: Carries space and closes windows in underneath coverage
use_when: Use when coverage grade and range traits are clear
avoid_when: Do not use for pressure-only hybrids
sample_phrase: Coverage range gives him value beyond the box because he can carry space and close throwing lanes.

plain_english: Adds value as a blitzer or pressure mover
use_when: Use when blitz or pass-rush impact is real
avoid_when: Do not use for pure off-ball types with no pressure history
sample_phrase: Pressure utility shows up when he is sent or moved because he times entry and finishes through contact.

plain_english: Takes on blocks squarely and discards them to stay clean
use_when: Use when the linebacker consistently defeats second-level blockers
avoid_when: Do not use when the player survives mostly by slipping blocks
sample_phrase: Can stack and shed instead of staying blocked
notes: which keeps the run-fit clean through traffic.

plain_english: Sees the rep early enough to trigger before the alley fully opens
use_when: Use when processing and range combine to make the player look faster in pads than on a stopwatch
avoid_when: Do not use for athletes who are late to diagnose
sample_phrase: Play speed shows up because he gets the picture sorted quickly and beats blockers to the spot.

plain_english: Discourages throws through coverage quality and leverage control
use_when: Use when snaps per target and target suppression support it
avoid_when: Do not use when the player is attacked often
sample_phrase: Target deterrence is part of the value because quarterbacks are not living on clean access throws into his area.

plain_english: Finds and finishes on the football
use_when: Use when PBUs
avoid_when: forced incompletion
sample_phrase: or INT profile supports it
notes: Do not use when ball production is weak
column12: Ball disruption shows up at the catch point with timing

plain_english: Can function across man
use_when: zone
avoid_when: deep
sample_phrase: and slot responsibilities
notes: Use when man-zone versatility is real
column12: Do not use for role-locked DBs
column13: Scheme translation is cleaner because the profile survives more than one coverage family.

plain_english: Pursuit and finish angles can break down
use_when: Use when missed tackles or run-support issues persist
avoid_when: Do not use when tackling is a clear strength
sample_phrase: Angle discipline and finish consistency still need to tighten when the rep spills into space.

plain_english: Transitions cleanly and drives downhill on the throw
use_when: Use when the corner or safety can trigger out of pedal or off leverage without wasted steps
avoid_when: Do not use for linear athletes who need too much gather to redirect
sample_phrase: Click-and-close ability shows up when the route breaks and he can plant
column12: and arrive on the football without losing balance.

plain_english: Movement skill shows up in response to route stimulus
use_when: Use when the player’s coverage movement is more impressive in live reaction than in straight testing
avoid_when: Do not use as a generic athletic compliment
sample_phrase: Reactive athleticism shows up when the route changes picture and he still matches it without panic or wasted motion.

plain_english: Loose and fluid hips for smooth transitions through route changes
use_when: Use privately when evaluating corners or safeties with exceptional transition fluidity
avoid_when: Do not use on public cards because the phrase is slang-heavy
sample_phrase: Transition fluidity is a real strength and shows up when he opens

plain_english: Sudden mover with quick first-step burst and redirection
use_when: Use when short-area movement is a true weapon
avoid_when: Do not use as a substitute for functional processing or play speed
sample_phrase: Twitch shows up early in the rep and forces defenders to react to his sudden movement profile.

plain_english: Competent player without difference-making traits
use_when: Use privately when the player is functional but lacks any real separator traits
avoid_when: Do not use on public cards because it reads dismissive and unpolished
sample_phrase: The projection looks more steady than difference-making unless one part of the profile takes a real jump.

plain_english: Work ethic
use_when: coachability
avoid_when: and love of the process
sample_phrase: Use privately when building internal confidence on developmental outcomes
notes: Do not surface publicly unless you have direct sourced reporting and legal comfort
column12: The work habits and day-to-day approach support the player lasting in an NFL building.
